hypothesis: Contrastive alignment between SMILES and 3D electron density grids implicitly encodes stereochemical information such that extending the pretraining framework to include enantiomeric pairs—where the same canonical SMILES maps to both R and S conformers as distinct electron density inputs—will force the model to develop chirality-sensitive embeddings that separate mirror-image molecules in latent space without any explicit stereochemical supervision, thereby resolving the current limitation that rigid SO(3) invariance conflates enantiomers that differ only in handedness.
